Klamath Basin Salmon Spawning

Badhia Yunes Katz - FlowWest

May 13, 2026

About This Document

This document records where and when salmon spawn in the Klamath River basin using only the sources listed in the Source Inventory.

Basin Overview

Tributary locations

River km values from Perry et al. (2019), p. 5.

Tributary locations. Source: Perry et al. (2019) [PERRY-2019], p. 5.
Tributary River km State Video data in hand
Bogus Creek 311.6 CA ✅ 2007–2022
Shasta River 289.6 CA ✅ 2006–2022
Scott River 232.8 CA ✅ 2008–2022
Salmon River 107.5 CA ⬜ Not yet collected
Trinity River 70.6 CA ⬜ Not yet collected

What the video counts measure: Each row in the raw data is one fish observed on an underwater camera, classified by species and direction. Upstream (U) = fish entering the tributary to spawn. Annual upstream counts are a passage index — not a complete escapement estimate — but give precise run timing unavailable from any other source in hand.

Upstream passage observations by tributary and species. Source: [CDFW-VIDEO]. Derived from cleaned CSV files.
Tributary Species Years Survey years Total upstream obs

Shasta River Coho 2006 – 2022 17 1,369
Shasta River Steelhead 2006 – 2022 17 2,961

Bogus Creek

River km: 311.6 | State: CA | Monitoring: CDFW Iron Gate Hatchery

Bogus Creek enters the Klamath 0.4 km below Iron Gate Dam and receives some of the earliest-arriving fall Chinook in the system. It is one of three upstream source populations in the Perry et al. (2019) S3 model.

Hatchery note: Iron Gate Hatchery fish receive adipose fin clips. Adipose clip rates in carcass surveys range 0–47% across years — high-clip years indicate elevated hatchery contribution and should be interpreted cautiously for natural spawner abundance.

Fall Chinook — run timing

Bogus Creek fall Chinook upstream passage. Source: [CDFW-VIDEO]. Peak week = calendar week with highest daily upstream count. Years with fewer than 20 upstream fish excluded.
Year Upstream count First fish Peak week Last fish
2007 4,483 Sep 25 Oct 14 Dec 04
2008 5,134 Sep 08 Oct 12 Nov 20
2009 6,268 Sep 08 Oct 11 Dec 13
2010 4,305 Sep 07 Oct 17 Dec 11
2011 9,018 Sep 13 Oct 16 Dec 13
2012 13,376 Sep 09 Oct 14 Nov 30
2013 3,535 Sep 17 Oct 20 Dec 03
2014 13,890 Sep 16 Oct 19 Dec 07
2015 2,879 Sep 16 Oct 11 Nov 25
2016 1,365 Sep 17 Oct 09 Nov 27
2017 3,664 Sep 12 Oct 15 Nov 28
2018 4,118 Sep 12 Oct 21 Dec 07
2019 1,626 Sep 21 Oct 13 Dec 11
2020 3,128 Sep 23 Oct 18 Jan 12
2021 2,917 Sep 21 Oct 17 Dec 03
2022 2,781 Sep 23 Oct 23 Nov 28

Bogus Creek fall Chinook run timing. Dot = peak week (size = annual count). Bar = full run window. Source: [CDFW-VIDEO].

Timing pattern: Peak upstream Chinook passage at Bogus Creek falls consistently in mid-October (Oct 10–24 across 2007–2022). First fish arrive early-to-mid September; run concludes late November to early December.

Shasta River

River km: 289.6 | State: CA | Monitoring: CDFW Region 1

The Shasta River confluence at rkm 289.6 marks the upstream boundary of the Ceratonova shasta infectious zone (rkm 289.6–213.0; Perry et al. 2019, p. 27). It is one of two primary coho natal tributaries in Dodrill et al. (2022).

Fall Chinook — run timing

Shasta River fall Chinook upstream passage. Source: [CDFW-VIDEO]. Peak week consistently last week of September to first week of October — 2–4 weeks earlier than Bogus Creek and Scott River.

Coho — adult returns and run timing

Adult return estimates are exact values from Table 2, Dodrill et al. (2022), p. 6, citing CDFW. Peak immigration timing mid-November to early December with less inter-annual variation than Scott River (Dodrill et al. 2022, Fig. 5B).

Shasta River adult coho returns. Source: Table 2, Dodrill et al. (2022) [DODRILL-2022], p. 6.

2008 30
2009 9 Critically low
2010 44
2011 62
2012 114
2013 163

Scott River

River km: 232.8 | State: CA | Monitoring: SRWC; CDFW Region 1

The Scott River is excluded from Perry et al. (2019) calibration — it enters below the Kinsman trap (Perry et al. 2019, p. 23). It is one of two primary coho natal tributaries in Dodrill et al. (2022).

Fall Chinook — run timing

Scott River fall Chinook upstream passage. Source: [CDFW-VIDEO]. Peak passage mid-to-late October. Low-count years (2015: 406; 2016: 395; 2018: 283; 2022: 70) indicate poor run years.

Coho — adult returns and run timing

Adult return estimates from Table 2, Dodrill et al. (2022), p. 6. Peak immigration timing showed considerable inter-annual variation, ranging from mid-October to late December (Dodrill et al. 2022, Fig. 5A).

Scott River — Coho Salmon Spawning

Source: Scott River Watershed Council annual spawning ground surveys [SRWC-2024], [SRWC-2025]. Coho only. Survey method: walk surveys, redd counting. A redd = nest excavated by a female, confirming spawning at that location.

Season summary

Season Survey period Miles surveyed Total redds Spawner estimate
2022-2023 Jan 4–27, 2023 1.45 (4 reaches only) 5 236 (CDFW weir)
2023-2024 Nov 28, 2023–Jan 16, 2024 38.3 251 912 (preliminary)
2024-2025 Nov 25, 2024–Jan 16, 2025 28.7 111 1,052 (preliminary)

2022-2023 is an acknowledged undercount — high flows pushed fish into unsurveyed areas. Do not use for trend comparisons.

Where spawning occurs

How spatial distribution has shifted

When spawning occurs

Season First redds Peak spawning Last live fish
2023-2024 Nov 28, 2023 (Scott R. near Sugar Ck) Mid-Dec to early Jan Jan 16, 2024
2024-2025 Nov 25, 2024 (French Ck, Sugar Ck) Mid-Dec Jan 16, 2025

First coho at CDFW weir (rkm 29.2): Oct 30–Nov 5, 2024. Peak weir passage: Nov 15–20. First redds appear approximately 5–10 days after peak weir passage. Spawning window approximately 10 weeks.

Mainstem Klamath River — Fall Chinook

Source: Perry et al. (2019) [PERRY-2019]

Spawning confined to rkm 178–312. Most redds concentrated immediately below Iron Gate Dam (Perry et al. 2019, p. 36).

Female spawner counts and timing

Exact values from Table 6, p. 37, citing Gough & Som (2015). Covers rkm 236–312 (Iron Gate Dam to Kinsman trap).

Female fall Chinook spawners in mainstem Klamath River (rkm 236–312). Peak spawning (50th percentile) consistently Oct 19–Nov 8. Source: Table 6, Perry et al. (2019) [PERRY-2019], p. 37. Series ends at brood year 2012.

Mainstem fall Chinook peak spawning window by brood year. Dot = 50th percentile. Bar = 20th–80th percentile. Source: Table 6, Perry et al. (2019).

Sources Appendix

ID Full citation
[PERRY-2019] Perry RW, Plumb JM, Jones EC, Som NA, Hardy TB, Hetrick NJ (2019). Application of the Stream Salmonid Simulator (S3) to Klamath River fall Chinook salmon, California — Parameterization and calibration. USGS Open-File Report 2019-1107
[DODRILL-2022] Dodrill MJ, Perry RW, Som NA, Manhard CV, Alexander JD (2022). Extending the Stream Salmonid Simulator to accommodate the life history of coho salmon in the Klamath River Basin, Northern California. USGS Open-File Report 2022-1071
[CDFW-VIDEO] California Department of Fish and Wildlife — Klamath River Project. DMP underwater video passage data: Bogus Creek (2003–2022), Shasta River (1999–2022), Scott River (2007–2022). Obtained via data.ca.gov — Fall Chinook Escapement, Klamath Basin Watershed. Available at: California Open Data Portal
[SRWC-2025] Scott River Watershed Council. Scott River Coho Salmon Spawning Ground Surveys: 2024–2025 Season. Etna, California, 2025. Available at: PDF
[SRWC-2024] Scott River Watershed Council. Scott River Spawning Ground Survey Report: 2023–2024 Season. Etna, California, 2024. Available at: PDF
[SRWC-2023] Scott River Watershed Council. Scott River Spawning Ground Survey Report: 2022–2023 Season. Etna, California, 2024. Available at: PDF
